notes: Answers are grounded in repository code and docs where visible.
This is not a case study narrative; it is structured extraction material.

1_executive_summary.what_is_the_project: NINA is a production-oriented voice navigation platform that lets websites add safe, context-aware voice control through an embeddable SDK, a deterministic FastAPI decision engine, and a generated per-site agent.json contract.
1_executive_summary.what_problem_does_it_solve: It bridges natural voice commands to reliable website execution by converting speech into typed instructions (navigate/search/click/needs_login/no_match) rather than brittle free-form browser automation.
1_executive_summary.who_uses_it: Primary users are website teams integrating voice navigation and end users interacting hands-free through the Nina widget on integrated sites.
1_executive_summary.why_does_it_matter: It matters because most voice web automation breaks in production due to selector drift, auth gates, and ambiguous intent; NINA adds deterministic resolution and safety controls to make voice interactions operationally viable.
1_executive_summary.what_makes_it_unique: It combines one LLM parse step with deterministic contract-based execution, plus runtime selector failure reporting and structured recovery/healing pathways.
1_executive_summary.final_outcome: A working three-package system (generator, API, SDK) with evaluators/tests, deployable infrastructure patterns, and reusable onboarding flow for site-level voice enablement.

2_problem_discovery.pain_point: There was no scalable, safe, reusable method to execute natural voice commands across arbitrary websites, especially across auth and dynamic UI changes.
existing_solutions_evaluated: LLM-heavy direct DOM control without deterministic site contract
2_problem_discovery.why_insufficient: These were either brittle at runtime, unsafe for production, difficult to scale across sites, or weak at deterministic reproducibility.
2_problem_discovery.core_insight: Constrain LLM to intent extraction and move action execution to deterministic resolver + guardrails against a validated per-site contract (agent.json).
constraints: Need for graceful degraded modes without full infra

3_product_vision.original_vision: Universal voice navigation for websites via simple SDK embed and generated site map.
3_product_vision.vision_changes: Expanded from simple command routing to production-oriented page-aware/session-aware orchestration with explicit recovery, guardrails, and planner capabilities.
assumptions_wrong: Single-shot parsing plus static selectors would be enough in production
Basic context without explicit page/session modeling would remain reliable
assumptions_correct: LLM parse + deterministic execution split improves safety and explainability
Typed instruction contracts are superior to free-form tool outputs for browser execution

4_system_architecture.high_level_architecture: Three layers: nina-generator (offline site intelligence generation), nina-api (runtime intent-to-instruction control plane), nina-sdk (browser voice capture and DOM execution plane).
4_system_architecture.data_flow: Speech -> transcript -> API query payload with page/session context -> parse -> resolve -> guardrail/auth gate -> typed instruction -> SDK execution -> optional selector failure report -> recovery instruction -> async analytics logging.
4_system_architecture.control_flow: Policy-first: validate/rate-limit/load-site -> parse intent -> deterministic resolve -> safety checks -> auth gate -> return instruction; on execution failure branch to recovery matrix.
4_system_architecture.request_lifecycle: SDK sends QueryRequest, API performs checks and context assembly, parser returns intent, resolver maps instruction, guardrails decide allow/block, response returned, SDK executes, logs written async.

state_management.browser_state: Widget state + sessionStorage queue + session ID
state_management.backend_state: SessionState semantic memory + action log + observation history

4_system_architecture.storage_strategy: Supabase as source of truth for site metadata/config and query telemetry; generated agent manifests created offline and uploaded.
4_system_architecture.caching_strategy: Redis-backed key TTL caches with in-memory fallback (agent config, rate counters, session/cache keys).
4_system_architecture.authentication_strategy: Site API key issuance (one-time plaintext display), hash-at-rest lookup, runtime auth gating via requires_auth/needs_login flow.
4_system_architecture.deployment_architecture: Containerized FastAPI behind Nginx TLS reverse proxy; env-configured external services (Groq, Supabase, optional Redis); SDK built and served separately.
architecture_diagrams: User -> SDK (voice + page context) -> POST /api/v1/query -> API pipeline (security -> cache/db -> parser -> resolver -> guardrails -> auth gate) -> typed instruction -> SDK executor
SDK selector failure -> POST /api/v1/report-broken-selector -> recovery classifier/matrix -> recovery instruction -> SDK
Generator inputs (URL/docs/sitemap/manifest) -> parser/crawler/intent-gen/assembler/validator -> agent.json -> sites API upload

5_agent_architecture.agent_count_model: Single constrained decision agent pattern with orchestrated submodules (not multi-agent framework).

agent_responsibilities.parser_agent_role: Interpret user intent into structured schema

5_agent_architecture.agent_communication: Typed in-process handoffs and HTTP JSON contracts between SDK and backend.
5_agent_architecture.handoffs: IntentResult -> ResolvedIntent -> QueryResponse -> SDK executor; SelectorFailureReport -> recovery response.
5_agent_architecture.failure_handling: Parser fallback to unknown, guardrail block to no_match, recovery matrix for selector/runtime issues, queue/auth replay for gated actions.
5_agent_architecture.retry_handling: Bounded retries in SDK API client and recovery logic; hard abort for non-retry-safe failures.
5_agent_architecture.confidence_scoring: Model-provided intent confidence gated by rule thresholds; separate healer confidence controls auto-commit vs review.
5_agent_architecture.drift_prevention: Deterministic agent.json-constrained resolution plus semantic/risk checks before execution.
5_agent_architecture.infinite_loop_prevention: Iteration caps, stagnation detection, retry ceilings, queue depth/TTL limits, and explicit abort paths.

6_technical_decisions.rejection_reasons: Rejected due to safety, brittleness, weak adaptability, and poor production reproducibility.
technical_debt: Advanced healing/visual recovery not fully wired into hot path
In-memory fallback state not ideal for horizontal scale
Secret hygiene/process debt in env handling
redesign_today: Shared API-SDK schema source with contract CI tests
Activate and productionize true LLM provider failover
Feature-flagged rollout controls for healing/visual recovery

top_10_hardest_problems: Generator input normalization across messy formats
7_engineering_challenges.how_solved: Solved with layered deterministic architecture, schema contracts, guardrails, recovery matrix, session memory, planner kill switches, and validation/eval tooling.
unexpected_bugs: Legacy schema anomalies (pages object, keywords key, nested actions)
Potential dist artifact drift relative to SDK src
production_incidents: Upstream model/network transient classes likely occurred
[CLARIFY: Explicit incident timeline and severity data not present in repository.]
scalability_issues: In-memory fallback consistency limits across replicas

models_used: qwen-qwq-32b via Groq (primary in parser and generator intent generation)
8_ai_architecture.model_choice_rationale: Optimized for low-latency structured intent parsing rather than long-form generation.
8_ai_architecture.prompting_strategy: Schema-constrained prompts with trust separation (user intent high-trust, browser snapshot low-trust) and strict JSON output.
8_ai_architecture.rag_strategy: No vector RAG; contextual grounding comes from agent.json + page_context + AXTree-like snapshot + session memory.
8_ai_architecture.fallback_strategy: Unknown-intent fallback, guardrail no_match fallback, deterministic resolver coverage, recovery branch for runtime failures.
8_ai_architecture.evaluation_strategy: Categorized eval suites with expected instruction assertions, mock-mode regression checks, and optional real-model runs.
8_ai_architecture.hallucination_prevention: LLM cannot execute actions directly; deterministic resolver enforces contract-bound actions.
latency_optimizations: Single inference call on hot path

9_voice_architecture.speech_to_text_flow: Browser Web Speech API captures audio and emits interim/final transcript events to SDK pipeline.
9_voice_architecture.text_to_speech_flow: No synthesized TTS pipeline in current code; spoken responses represented as UI messages.
9_voice_architecture.streaming_architecture: Interim transcript updates are local-stream-like, backend processing remains per-utterance HTTP request/response.
9_voice_architecture.latency_targets: [CLARIFY: No explicit formal SLO values committed in repository; architecture tuned for interactive sub-second to low-second behavior.]
9_voice_architecture.voice_interruption_handling: Mic state machine, silence timeout stop, and hotkey press/release control prevent overlap.
9_voice_architecture.error_recovery: User-friendly STT error mapping, timeout handling, retry-once API calls, and downstream selector-failure escalation.
9_voice_architecture.fallback_behavior: Safe no_match with suggestions and auth-aware queue replay for blocked actions.
9_voice_architecture.realtime_synchronization: SDK state machines plus mutation observers and wait predicates synchronize execution and UI/page state.

10_security.authentication: Hashed API-key authentication with one-time key reveal at registration.
10_security.authorization: Action-level requires_auth gating with needs_login response flow and replay.
10_security.rate_limiting: Per-window counters with Redis preferred and in-memory fallback.
10_security.input_validation: Pydantic schemas, key format checks, transcript bounds, JSON parsing guards, manifest validation.
10_security.abuse_prevention: Credential/prompt-injection detection, risk gating, semantic mismatch blocking, dangerous selector rejection.
10_security.secrets_management: [CLARIFY: Repository includes .env with key-like value; operationally this indicates inadequate secret hygiene for source control and requires rotation + secret scanning enforcement.]
10_security.data_protection: PII scrubbing in logs (emails, phones, cards, SSNs), no password handling automation, and constrained execution boundaries.

11_analytics.why: To measure resolution quality, diagnose failure patterns, and prioritize agent.json or parser improvements.
11_analytics.insights_discovered: [CLARIFY: Analytics schema and endpoints exist; specific quantified insight reports are not included in repo artifacts.]
11_analytics.impact_on_decisions: Design and validation emphasis on fallback suggestions, intent coverage expansion, and selector recovery pathways implies analytics-informed iteration.

12_deployment.hosting: VPS deployment guide with Nginx+Certbot and optional Railway deployment instructions.
12_deployment.monitoring: Structured JSON logs + endpoint checks; no full APM integration defined in-repo.
12_deployment.ci_cd: [CLARIFY: No explicit CI pipeline config files surfaced in current snapshot.]
12_deployment.logging: Structured JSON logging formatter in backend and container/Nginx log guidance.
12_deployment.error_tracking: App-level exception handlers and logs; no dedicated external error SaaS integration visible.
12_deployment.scaling_strategy: Scale API statelessly, externalize session/cache state, improve provider failover, and increase log pipeline robustness.

13_metrics.requests: [CLARIFY: No total request volume report included.]
13_metrics.latency: Latency fields captured per query and eval scripts report average ms.
13_metrics.accuracy: Eval scripts and docs reference 90%+ class outcomes depending on mode.
13_metrics.agent_success_rate: Equivalent runtime metric represented as resolved/resolution rate via analytics summary endpoint.
13_metrics.cost_reductions: [CLARIFY: No audited cost report in repository.]
13_metrics.time_savings: Integration model (script tag + generated manifest) implies major setup-time reduction vs bespoke implementations.
13_metrics.productivity_gains: Generator + validator + eval tooling reduce manual mapping/debug burden and increase repeatability.

biggest_mistakes: Underestimating schema inconsistency from upstream inputs
what_do_differently: Contract CI checks for SDK/API parity
Early secret scanning and strict env handling
Earlier distributed session strategy for scale
14_lessons_learned.most_valuable_lesson: Model intelligence must be bounded by deterministic contracts and explicit safety policy to be production-trustworthy.
unexpected_discoveries: Input normalization quality directly controls runtime reliability
Failure recovery UX is a core product feature, not just an ops concern
engineering_principles_learned: Keep execution contracts typed and testable
Treat runtime drift as first-class architecture input

what_comes_next: Strengthen distributed state and provider failover
Expand multilingual and domain-specific intent quality
planned_features: Richer analytics and per-category quality dashboards
Stronger CI contract and artifact consistency checks
planned_architecture_improvements: Single source of truth schema for SDK/API contracts
if_funded_evolution: Robust observability stack (trace IDs, APM, SLO dashboards)
Enterprise-grade security posture (key management, abuse controls, audit trails)
